title: Cirrhopetalum lepidum
intro: Cirrhopetalum lepidum is a species of orchid, a type of flowering plant belonging to the family Orchidaceae. The health benefits of Cirrhopetalum lepidum include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various diseases. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Cirrhopetalum lepidum is used in herbal preparations, such as teas and tinctures, to support immune function and overall well-being.
taxonomy: Cirrhopetalum lepidum is classified within the plant family Orchidaceae. This family includes approximately 30,000 species of orchids, making it one of the largest and most diverse plant families. As a member of the Orchidaceae family, Cirrhopetalum lepidum falls under the subfamily Epidendroideae. Within the subfamily Epidendroideae, Cirrhopetalum lepidum is a part of the tribe Vandeae. The tribe Vandeae is further divided into various genera, with Cirrhopetalum being one of them.
